named_technique: Mignotte–Waldschmidt two-log gap bound + base-7 equidistribution bridge
bridge_lemma: By the digit recursion d^k·B_d = {0,d^k} + d^{k+1}·B_d, the k=2 sumset is the subset-sum set of the atoms {3^j,4^j,7^j : j≥2}; below N₀=3,982,888 cofiniteness is a finite verified check (5,207 misses, gap-free tail). Above N₀, each n=u+c with u∈B₃+B₄ and c a base-7 power-sum: the B₃+B₄ gaps grow ∝ top power but the ~log₇(n) available base-7 shifts jointly cover each gap, the worst single near-coincidences being controlled by the Mignotte–Waldschmidt bound |3^p−4^q| ≥ 3^p·exp(−C(log p)²). The MW input is k-uniform (level-2 uses only the pairs p,q≥2, a subset of the level-1 close pairs).
informal_proof_outline:
Target: every n > 3,982,888 is in R := 3²·B₃ + 4²·B₄ + 7²·B₇, where B_d = base-d 0/1-digit numbers.

L0 (atom form). The digit recursion d^k·B_d = {0,d^k} + d^{k+1}·B_d (a 0/1-digit number has units digit 0 or 1) iterates to: R = subset-sums of the atom set A = {3^j, 4^j, 7^j : j ≥ 2}, each atom used at most once. Elementary, Finset/Nat arithmetic.

L1 (finite covering below N₀ — VERIFIED, bit-exact). By exact atom-sieve to a bound ≫ N₀, the set of non-representable n is finite with largest element N₀(2) = 3,982,888; there are exactly 5,207 misses below it, and the tail (N₀, bound] is gap-free (verified with a long covered run, two independent engines bit-for-bit). So the theorem reduces to: no miss occurs above N₀.

L2 (pairwise gap control, k-uniform — Mignotte–Waldschmidt). The largest hole is NOT controlled by elementary density (the naive merged-atom interval-filling test under-predicts N₀ by 200×–29000×); it is pinned by how close cross-base powers can be. By MW two-log (Mignotte–Waldschmidt, Acta Arith. 53 (1989), Cor 10.1; the form BEGL96 cite for the k=1 hole 581): |3^p − 4^q| ≥ 3^p·exp(−C(log p)²) for an effective C, and likewise for the other base pairs. The close pairs are sparse and structured — p ∈ {5,19,24,29,34,…} spaced by the convergent denominators of log4/log3 ≈ 0.7925 — and are k-INDEPENDENT: at level k=2 only pairs with p,q ≥ 2 are relevant, a subset of the level-1 pairs. So the k=1 gap-control lifts verbatim to k=2 (the recursion L0 tracks the scale factor).

L3 (above-N₀ closure — the base-7 equidistribution bridge; COMPRESSED in the 1996 precedent, construct explicitly). For n > N₀ write n = u + c with u ∈ B₃+B₄ and c a base-7 subset-sum. The two-ray set B₃+B₄ has gaps growing ∝ top power (so no two-ray elementary argument closes it), but the third ray B₇ bridges every gap: there are ~log₇(n) available base-7 shifts, each covering ≈77% of any given B₃+B₄ gap, and their UNION closes the gap provided different shifts hit different sub-regions. This is a JOINT density-overlap / equidistribution property of the base-7 shift family modulo the 3-/4-power gap structure (the safety margin (shifts available)/(shifts needed) stays ~1.5–3×, flat — a tight, criticality-grade inequality). MW dispatches the finitely many worst single near-coincidences (e.g. the tightest triple 3^5≈4^4≈7^3); the asymptotic closure above N₀ is the equidistribution statement, which the 1996 precedent treats compactly. Aristotle should construct L3 explicitly (an exponential-sum / Weyl equidistribution or an exhaustive shift-union covering argument).

Conclusion. L1 (finite, verified) + L2 (MW, k-uniform pairwise control) + L3 (base-7 union covers every gap above N₀) give R ⊇ (N₀, ∞). ∎
candidate_lemmas: digit_recursion: d^k·B_d = {0,d^k} + d^{k+1}·B_d; iterating, R = subset-sums of {3^j,4^j,7^j : j≥2}.
finite_threshold: the non-representable set of R is finite with max N₀(2)=3,982,888 (5,207 misses, gap-free tail) — exact, bit-verified to a bound ≫ N₀.
mw_two_log: |3^p − 4^q| ≥ 3^p·exp(−C(log p)²) for effective C (Mignotte–Waldschmidt 1989, Cor 10.1); likewise for |3^p−7^l|, |4^q−7^l|.
close_pairs_k_uniform: the cross-base near-coincidence pairs are p∈{5,19,24,29,34,…} (convergents of log4/log3), independent of k; level k=2 uses only the subset with p,q≥2.
two_ray_gaps_grow: B₃+B₄ has gaps growing ∝ the top power, so no two-ray elementary (interval-filling) argument closes them; the third ray is necessary.
base7_shift_count: the number of available base-7 subset-sum shifts ≤ n grows ~ n^{log_7 2} ≈ n^0.356, giving ~log₇(n) usable shifts per gap.

joint_overlap_bridge: the union of available base-7 shifts covers every B₃+B₄ gap above N₀ (each shift covers ~77% of a gap; the union closes it by equidistribution of the shift family modulo the gap structure).
honest_disclaimer: PARTIAL / RESOLUTION-GRADE TARGET for a FIXED triple and FIXED k. This is the (3,4,7), k=2 instance ONLY — NOT the uniform BEGL96 conjecture (all admissible D, all k), which remains open. Status of the three legs: L1 (finite covering below N₀=3,982,888) is VERIFIED bit-exact (5,207 misses, gap-free tail, two independent engines; authors: the day-1 computational squad). L2 (pairwise MW gap control) is sound and k-uniform — it uses the SAME Mignotte–Waldschmidt two-log bound BEGL96 cited for the k=1 hole 581, restricted to powers ≥2 (dissection: matveev). L3 (above-N₀ closure via the base-7 equidistribution bridge) is the COMPRESSED step: the 1996 precedent treats the analogous k=1 closure compactly, and the honest mechanism is a JOINT equidistribution / density-overlap of the base-7 shift family, NOT a single pairwise alignment — Aristotle should construct L3 explicitly (exponential-sum/Weyl or exhaustive shift-union covering). Independent bit-exact verification of N₀ and the miss count by nesterenko. NOVELTY: BEGL96 published only k=1; the 124.lean all-k tag is uncited; citer sweeps found no k=2 proof. If Aristotle CANNOT close L3 unconditionally, a CONDITIONAL result (cofiniteness above N₀ MODULO the finite verification + a stated equidistribution lemma) is still informative and is the honest fallback. Role: ADJUDICATION — verify the legs, construct or flag L3.
